title: SOLVED Tracks Length
wikitext:
[]: [SOLVED] Tracks Length             Quote Post by Marth » Fri Jul 05, 2013 4:59 am Hello, I am so fond of evangelion music and recently I obtained LOSSLESS version of the soundtrack but the first track zankoku na tenshi no these (Ayanami version) last 5:16 min instead of 4:46. It contains Jikai Yokoku the 30 seconds version before Ayanami starts singing and so I wondered whether the track is original and that the true song does indeed start with Jikai Yokoku (which is the song to preview a next episode in the serie) or not? I have the same issue with the Angel Attack song of the "the day of the second impact" album. The song last 2:47 instead of 2:35 and contains song of an angel incoming before the song starts. Please I want only the person who have the physical version of the soundtracks to answer me. So what is the truth about these songs?

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Fri Jul 05, 2013 9:39 pm Hi, Marth: I in fact have the actual CD of The Birthday of Rei Ayanami (my wife bought this in Japan years ago, a good catch considering how rare it became). That's rather strange as on my CD it starts Track #1 (Zankoku...) with Rei-chan singing right away and not any interlude beforehand. The track length is 4:47 and the very last note rolls right into Track #2 "Rei I". I also have The Day of Second Impact and I can tell on that CD that Track #1 "Angel Attack" starts promptly with the song, the track lasting 2:30. There are some overlaps on Birthday between the tracks (it's really best listened to on one set) but not anything that's set as a prologue to any of the songs. That's funny that your version is somehow different.

[]:             Quote Post by Marth » Sat Jul 06, 2013 6:42 am Thank you a lot. Indeed the length difference was quite suspicious. I used Fission to trim the added part without altering the quality of the ALAC files. Thanks a lot anyway now they have a proper length of 4:46 and 2:32.

Article: SOLVED Tracks Length
[]: [SOLVED] Tracks Length             Quote Post by Marth » Fri Jul 05, 2013 4:59 am Hello, I am so fond of evangelion music and recently I obtained LOSSLESS version of the soundtrack but the first track zankoku na tenshi no these (Ayanami version) last 5:16 min instead of 4:46. It contains Jikai Yokoku the 30 seconds version before Ayanami starts singing and so I wondered whether the track is original and that the true song does indeed start with Jikai Yokoku (which is the song to preview a next episode in the serie) or not? I have the same issue with the Angel Attack song of the "the day of the second impact" album. The song last 2:47 instead of 2:35 and contains song of an angel incoming before the song starts. Please I want only the person who have the physical version of the soundtracks to answer me. So what is the truth about these songs?

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Fri Jul 05, 2013 9:39 pm Hi, Marth: I in fact have the actual CD of The Birthday of Rei Ayanami (my wife bought this in Japan years ago, a good catch considering how rare it became). That's rather strange as on my CD it starts Track #1 (Zankoku...) with Rei-chan singing right away and not any interlude beforehand. The track length is 4:47 and the very last note rolls right into Track #2 "Rei I". I also have The Day of Second Impact and I can tell on that CD that Track #1 "Angel Attack" starts promptly with the song, the track lasting 2:30. There are some overlaps on Birthday between the tracks (it's really best listened to on one set) but not anything that's set as a prologue to any of the songs. That's funny that your version is somehow different.

[]:             Quote Post by Marth » Sat Jul 06, 2013 6:42 am Thank you a lot. Indeed the length difference was quite suspicious. I used Fission to trim the added part without altering the quality of the ALAC files. Thanks a lot anyway now they have a proper length of 4:46 and 2:32.
